title: De Gustibus --
content:
В В В В Your ghost will walk, you lover of trees,
В В В В (If our loves remain)
В В В В In an English lane,
В В В В By a cornfield-side a-flutter with poppies.
В В В В Hark, those two in the hazel coppice
В В В В A boy and a girl, if the good fates please,
В В В В Making love, say,
В В В В The happier they!
В В В В Draw yourself up from the light of the moon,
В В В В And let them pass, as they will too soon,
В В В В With the bean-flowersпїЅ boon,
В В В В And the blackbirdпїЅs tune,
В В В В And May, and June!


В В В В What I love best in all the world
В В В В Is a castle, precipice-encurled,
В В В В In a gash of the wind-grieved Apennine
В В В В Or look for me, old fellow of mine,
В В В В (If I get my head from out the mouth
В В В В OпїЅ the grave, and loose my spiritпїЅs bands,
В В В В And come again to the land of lands)
В В В В In a sea-side house to the farther South,
В В В В Where the baked cicala dies of drouth,
В В В В And one sharp tree пїЅtis a cypress stands,
В В В В By the many hundred years red-rusted,
В В В В Rough iron-spiked, ripe fruit-oпїЅercrusted,
В В В В My sentinel to guard the sands
В В В В To the waterпїЅs edge. For, what expands
В В В В Before the house, but the great opaque
В В В В Blue breadth of sea without a break?
В В В В While, in the house, for ever crumbles
В В В В Some fragment of the frescoed walls,
В В В В From blisters where a scorpion sprawls.
В В В В A girl bare-footed brings, and tumbles
В В В В Down on the pavement, green-flesh melons,
В В В В And says thereпїЅs news to-day the king
В В В В Was shot at, touched in the liver-wing,
В В В В Goes with his Bourbon arm in a sling:
В В В В She hopes they have not caught the felons.
В В В В Italy, my Italy!
В В В В Queen MaryпїЅs saying serves for me
В В В В (When fortuneпїЅs malice
В В В В Lost her, Calais)
В В В В Open my heart and you will see
В В В В Graved inside of it, пїЅItaly.пїЅ
В В В В Such lovers old are I and she:
В В В В So it always was, so it still shall be!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Browning represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Robert Browning's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
